description: This figure presents Geometrically Necessary Dislocation (GND) distribution characterizations of the Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel under different tensile strains (0%, 1%, 3%, 6%), derived from electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) measurements. Subfigures (a1)-(d1) show GND mappings of the austenitic phase, while (a2)-(d2) show GND mappings of the B2 phase at each corresponding strain, all with a 5 μm scale bar, and the color bar indicates ρ_GND ranging from 10^13 to 10^16 m^-2. Subfigures (e) and (f) are GND density frequency histograms for austenite and B2 phase, respectively, with Gaussian fitted peak values marked. Subfigure (g) displays the average ρ_GND of both phases as a function of strain. Key trends: Initial GND density of B2 (1.04×10^14 m^-2 at 0% strain) is higher than that of austenite (5.36×10^13 m^-2) due to smaller B2 grain size. Both phases show monotonically increasing GND density with rising strain, and austenite accumulates dislocations more rapidly, surpassing B2 density at 6% strain (2.64×10^14 m^-2 vs 2.51×10^14 m^-2). This confirms co-deformation of the two phases, with higher deformability of austenite, supporting the heterogeneous deformation induced strengthening and strain hardening mechanisms of the duplex steel.
